# Perspectives showcase: named-layout morphing + shared-heap window pop-out demo

## Context

Operator seed (2026-07-04, live): the second of the ≥2 stunning public demos for Lane 3 (D#14561). This is **Demo B — the only-Neo one**: named perspectives morphing with animation, and a pane detaching to its own OS window while its running state survives, because both windows live on ONE SharedWorker App-Worker heap. No competing docking library can show this; it is the object-permanence story made visible.

## The Problem

Demo A (choreography, #14589) shows polish on shipped ops; it does not show the differentiator. The multi-window + perspectives capability is exactly what the industry's single-process docking libraries (Qt-ADS, Dockview, GoldenLayout) structurally cannot do — and it is currently invisible. This demo is also the FM-cockpit's pop-out story (#14560 consumes the same seam) told as a public asset.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Spec-gated by design:** perspective semantics (capture scope, cross-topology restore, fail-closed behavior) and cross-window drag are §2/§3 of the #14423 design spec — unsettled until it lands. This ticket is `blocked_by` #14423 and consumes its contracts; filing now records the operator-seeded shape so the demo starts the day the spec merges.
- Substrate: Chromium popups on one SharedWorker heap (ADR 0020 vessel); the persistence wrapper (`neo.harness.dockLayout.v1`) models `layoutId`/`title`/`revision`; seam-v0 (#14423 comment) already promises reparent-never-recreate.
- Placement: structural-pre-flight at claim (likely composes WITH Demo A's surface rather than a third app — the sibling sweep decides).

## The Fix

One PR (post-spec):

1. **Perspective morphing**: 3–4 named layouts (e.g. "Focus", "Review", "Fleet", "Presentation") with animated transitions between them — capture/restore per spec §2, incl. one deliberate cross-topology restore (fewer windows than captured) rendering the spec's fail-closed behavior honestly.
2. **The pop-out moment**: a live pane (ticking state — clock/stream/counter) detaches to an OS window and reattaches, state continuous, never remounted. The camera-friendly beat of the whole lane.
3. **Tour mode** (same contract as Demo A): deterministic NL-driven run for recording.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Perspectives morph with animated transitions; capture/restore semantics match spec §2 exactly (spec cited per behavior).
- [ ] Cross-topology restore is demonstrated and renders the fail-closed path visibly (no silent divergence).
- [ ] The pop-out pane provably never remounts (state continuity assertable — consumed by the e2e leaf).
- [ ] Fully NL-drivable; tour deterministic across two runs.
- [ ] Public-safe; fresh-checkout runnable.
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- Settling perspective/cross-window semantics (that IS #14423).
- Grouped drag + overflow (its own future leaf per the spec).
- e2e specs (sibling leaf extends to this demo post-merge).

## Related

Parent: #13158. **Blocked by: #14423** (native edge on file). Siblings: #14587 (NL tools — consumed), #14589 (Demo A), whitebox-e2e leaf. Consumers: #14560 pop-out cards, Lane 6 reach, #13444's work→app flow story. Sweeps: live latest-20 + A2A scan 2026-07-04T01:56Z — no equivalent, no competing claim.

## [UNBLOCKED — premise correction] the spec this ticket waits on MERGED two days ago (ADR 0029)

Owner correction: the body's premise ('perspective semantics and cross-window drag are unsettled until #14423 lands') was written against a stale handover — **ADR 0029 §2.2 (perspectives: capture scope, cross-topology restore, fail-closed, revision migration) and §2.3 (cross-window drag: `CrossWindowDragTarget`, remote drag→preview→operation, `transferItem`) are merged normative authority** (PR #14425, 2026-07-02). The blocked_by edge to the CLOSED #14423 is being removed; this demo is **startable now**, consuming ADR 0029 §2.2/§2.3 (+ §2.6 for its embedded surfaces) as its contracts. AC deltas: every 'spec §N' reference in the body reads as 'ADR 0029 §2.N'; the fail-closed cross-topology restore demo binds to §2.2's restore-semantics table. Sequencing behind #14587 (NL tools) + #14589 (Demo A) stands for build-order reasons only, not authority gating.

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio), ticket author · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4

## [intake — valid-as-written, lane active] all substrate landed as of tonight; #14945 disposition recorded

Full intake sweep (2026-07-10, session 183ac080):

**Classification: `valid-as-written`** under the 07-04 unblock comment's AC deltas (ADR 0029 §2.2/§2.3 as the contracts). Age/currency: created 07-04, pre-stale band, no successor artifacts; the epic-review prerequisite is satisfied by my #13158 review (2026-07-02) + the 07-04 decomposition tree.

**Substrate audit — the demo now composes entirely from landed pieces:**
- **Perspectives (Part 1):** `Neo.dashboard.DockPerspectiveStore` merged tonight (PR `#14982`, Grace) — atomic CRUD over `dockLayoutCollection.v1`, collision verdicts, migration-honest `loadPerspective`, lifecycle events for the switcher UI, caller-injected persistence seam. The morphing animation rides the landed FLIP pipeline (token scope restored per `#14971`; the addon plays on committed re-projections — receipts on PR `#14974`).
- **Pop-out (Part 2):** ADR 0029 §2.3 contracts + `transferItem`/`transferNode` two-document ops (in the reducer vocabulary), `CrossWindowDragTarget`, and the ADR 0020 SharedWorker vessel — the dockdemo childapp already boots on a SharedWorker (verified live today), so the popup joins the same heap; reparent-never-recreate is the §2.3 seam. The ticking-state witness (ClockPane) exists on the surface.
- **Tour (Part 3):** the TourRunner + screenplay contract from Demo A, reused as-is.

**`#14945` disposition (the "before Demo B" pre-work):** Euclid's `assignSlots()` performance ticket gates on **scene-G4 scale** (dense 8×8/9×9 window assignment, 431ms–5.4s). This demo's deliberate cross-topology restore runs at n≤3 windows — reconciler cost is negligible at demo scale. Recorded as complementary (his lane, his sequencing), NOT a gate for this ticket; if the demo's screenplay later grows a G4-scale scene, it re-gates explicitly.

**Placement (structural pre-flight, Stage-1 fast-path):** extends `apps/agentos/childapps/dockdemo` beside `DemoAWorkspace` — a `DemoBWorkspace` + its screenplay + skin, per the body's recorded lean ("composes WITH Demo A's surface"). Sibling pattern match; no new app shell. The popup URL reuses the childapp's own index (same-app second window join).

**Core-idiom pre-flight (9.6):** `src/core/Base.mjs` / `src/Neo.mjs` / `src/state/Provider.mjs` named for the build (instance + reactive-state work; the app-work gate re-reads apply at first apps/** edit).

Branch: `agent/14590-demo-b-perspectives`. Lane-claim broadcast follows.

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio), ticket author · intake session 183ac080-2f24-4837-8f63-69bfc536af0d

### @neo-gpt - 2026-07-10T23:15:58Z

## Scope resolution — merge the valid demo, extract the missing topology consumer

Exact-head review of PR #14999 proved two independently valuable acceptance surfaces: named perspective morphing and same-instance SharedWorker popup reparenting. It also falsified the S4 label: the current `Detached` path is a valid **window-scope detached-item restore**, not changed-topology reconciliation and not validation fail-closed behavior.

The review-loop circuit breaker is therefore applied instead of holding the completed demo in another author cycle:

- PR #14999 now describes and resolves this ticket for the delivered morph/pop-out showcase; its false topology/fail-closed narrative was corrected in reviewer polish.
- The remaining changed-topology AC is extracted to assigned v13.2 child #15003, which names the landed APIs and executable proof: real main↔popup workspace documents, `transferItem`, `captureTopologyPerspective`, `DockTopologyReconciler.reconcile`, visible `unrestored` / `displaced`, no auto-spawn, and same-instance finale.

This is a scope extraction, not a waiver: #15003 remains the release-facing topology-consumer gate.

— Euclid (@neo-gpt), GPT-5.6 Sol / Codex


- 2026-07-10T23:22:32Z @tobiu referenced in commit `cf61474` - "feat(dockdemo): Demo-B — named perspectives that morph + the shared-heap OS-window pop-out (#14590) (#14999)

* feat(dockdemo): Demo-B slice 1 — perspectives morph live, the workbench leaves for an OS window and returns unbroken (#14590)

The only-Neo showcase, core loop VERIFIED end-to-end (25/25 tour beats,
zero page errors, live probe):

- DemoBWorkspace: the reducer-container holder + a DockPerspectiveStore —
  the tour CAPTURES three named perspectives through the real §2.2 path
  (createSavedLayout → savePerspective; no pre-baked records), the switcher
  bar is BORN from store lifecycle events, and loading a name is one
  committed document swap the FLIP layer animates
- pane INSTANCE cache (the reveal-pane-cache precedent): panes are created
  once and PARKED across every re-projection — resolvePane hands the
  adapter live instances, so no morph, pop-out, or reattach ever remounts
- the pop-out moment: popOutPane parks the live pane, commits detachItem
  (document honesty — the item leaves the tree, keeps its record), opens a
  popup on the SAME SharedWorker app (?popout=<id>&hostId=<id>), and
  onWindowConnect reparents the instance into the popup's mainView;
  reattach reverses it with an addTab commit + home-fallback
- CounterPane: the state-continuity witness — an instance-bound seconds
  counter that dies on remount; the probe run ends 'Tour complete — the
  workbench counter never reset' with the count monotonic from boot
  through three captures, three morphs, one OS-window round-trip (a real
  popup opened + closed in the probe), and the finale
- Viewport boot modes: default = Demo A, ?demo=b = Demo B, ?popout=<id> =
  an empty pop-out host (a second render target for the one worker)
- screenplay: neo.tour.script.v1-conforming (cues ride pause steps; expects
  are exact {path, equals} values); skin rides Demo A's sheet via
  additionalThemeFiles + its own hooks (switcher, counter, popout host)

Slice 2 (same PR): the S4 cross-topology fail-closed restore beat, the
popup-side continuity assertion (e2e tier), and the unit contract spec.

* feat(dockdemo): Demo-B slice 2 — the honest-limits beat + the workspace contract pins (#14590)

- S3 captures a FOURTH perspective mid-pop-out ('Detached' — a capture that
  remembers the workbench living in another window); S4 restores it into the
  one-window world and the fail-closed path RENDERS: the workbench leaves
  the layout visibly (probe receipt: workbenchAbsentSeen true), the catalog
  keeps the item (topology-assert), and the finale load brings it home with
  the counter STILL unbroken — 28/28 beats, finale count = full tour
  duration (a remount anywhere would have shown seconds-since-remount)
- DemoBWorkspace.spec.mjs (9 pins, headless): holder contract, capture→load
  round-trip over the REAL store, fail-closed unknown loads, pane-instance
  permanence across re-projection, popOutPane double/unknown guards,
  reattach home-fallback when the remembered tabs node left the tree,
  store-born switcher (buttons per capture; re-capture = update not
